title: Craft in the Real World
author: Matthew Salesses
subline: Rethinking writing, race, and the workshop.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A piece of lined paper. A typed paragraph. The names of expected protagonists are written in ash, but one of them is in RED — the unexpected character. The expectation lines are crossed out. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1100)" fill="none">
    <!-- Page boundary -->
    <rect x="-560" y="0" width="1120" height="1280" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <!-- 14 horizontal lines (lined paper) -->
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.4">
      <line x1="-500" y1="80" x2="500" y2="80"/>
      <line x1="-500" y1="160" x2="500" y2="160"/>
      <line x1="-500" y1="240" x2="500" y2="240"/>
      <line x1="-500" y1="320" x2="500" y2="320"/>
      <line x1="-500" y1="400" x2="500" y2="400"/>
      <line x1="-500" y1="480" x2="500" y2="480"/>
      <line x1="-500" y1="560" x2="500" y2="560"/>
      <line x1="-500" y1="640" x2="500" y2="640"/>
      <line x1="-500" y1="720" x2="500" y2="720"/>
      <line x1="-500" y1="800" x2="500" y2="800"/>
      <line x1="-500" y1="880" x2="500" y2="880"/>
      <line x1="-500" y1="960" x2="500" y2="960"/>
      <line x1="-500" y1="1040" x2="500" y2="1040"/>
      <line x1="-500" y1="1120" x2="500" y2="1120"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Three crossed-out words in ash (the old expectations) -->
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1.5">
      <text x="-500" y="100" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="32" font-weight="400" fill="#4c4c4c">HUSBAND</text>
      <line x1="-360" y1="80" x2="-200" y2="100" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="2"/>
      <text x="-500" y="180" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="32" font-weight="400" fill="#4c4c4c">WIFE</text>
      <line x1="-380" y1="160" x2="-280" y2="180" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="2"/>
      <text x="-500" y="260" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="32" font-weight="400" fill="#4c4c4c">SON</text>
      <line x1="-440" y1="240" x2="-360" y2="260" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="2"/>
    </g>
    <!-- The replacement word in RED -->
    <text x="-500" y="380" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="32" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46">HUMAN</text>
    <text x="-300" y="380" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="32" font-weight="400" fill="#4c4c4c">being</text>
  </g>
  <text x="800" y="2200" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.4em">THE READER IMAGINES A CHARACTER · THE WRITER SHOULD TOO</text>
</svg>
rationale: A page of lined paper. Three words are crossed out in ash — HUSBAND, WIFE, SON — the old Aristotelian expectations. The replacement word in red 32px caps is 'HUMAN.' The composition is a manuscript revision, and the single red word is the book's argument. Title 300px mixed case, two lines, weight 300.
